China is by far the largest producer of Barium (as barite) based on USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries 2024; data is recent and authoritative, reflecting over two-thirds of global supply.
India is the second-largest producer with well-documented mining operations and production statistics from national mineral reports and USGS data, very reliable.
Morocco holds significant barite reserves and has reliably reported production to international databases; data is fairly recent with minor reporting lags.
Mexico is a known mid-tier producer with production reported in USGS 2024 and national mining publications; reasonable confidence in figures.
Turkey is an important producer in the Mediterranean region with good data availability but slightly more limited compared to others, so moderate confidence.
